description:
As a benefits administrator
  I want to add employees
  So that new hires are included in payroll

steps.name: I am authenticated on the benefits dashboard
I add a new employee through the UI modal
the employee row should appear in the table with the expected values and business-rule calculations

the add modal fields should be empty

name: Add Employee

description:
As a QA engineer
  I want dashboard values to be accurate
  So that payroll data is trustworthy

steps.name: I am on the Paylocity login page
I login with configured credentials
I should see the benefits dashboard

elements.name: [UI-F-010] Calculations display correctly for known dependants

steps.name: I am authenticated on the benefits dashboard
payroll columns Gross Pay, Benefits Cost, and Net Pay should match expected business-rule values for that employee

elements.name: [UI-F-019] Payroll calculations are correct for accepted dependants limits

steps.name: I am authenticated on the benefits dashboard
an employee exists with dependants 0 via API
payroll columns Gross Pay, Benefits Cost, and Net Pay should match expected business-rule values for that employee

elements.name: [UI-F-019] Payroll calculations are correct for accepted dependants limits

steps.name: I am authenticated on the benefits dashboard
an employee exists with dependants 1 via API
payroll columns Gross Pay, Benefits Cost, and Net Pay should match expected business-rule values for that employee

elements.name: [UI-F-019] Payroll calculations are correct for accepted dependants limits

steps.name: I am authenticated on the benefits dashboard
an employee exists with dependants 31 via API
payroll columns Gross Pay, Benefits Cost, and Net Pay should match expected business-rule values for that employee

elements.name: [UI-F-019] Payroll calculations are correct for accepted dependants limits

steps.name: I am authenticated on the benefits dashboard
an employee exists with dependants 32 via API
payroll columns Gross Pay, Benefits Cost, and Net Pay should match expected business-rule values for that employee

elements.name: [UI-F-011] Header/value mapping for first and last name

steps.name: I am authenticated on the benefits dashboard
first and last name columns should map correctly for that employee

elements.name: [UI-F-014] Table reflects latest state after edit and delete actions

steps.name: I am authenticated on the benefits dashboard
I edit the existing employee through the UI modal
I delete the existing employee and confirm
the employee should not be visible in the table by id

name: Dashboard Data Validations

description:
As a benefits administrator
  I want to remove employees
  So that inactive records are no longer shown

steps.name: I am authenticated on the benefits dashboard
I delete the existing employee and confirm
the employee should not be visible in the table by id
I am authenticated on the benefits dashboard
I open delete and cancel for the existing employee
the employee should remain visible in the table by id

name: Delete Employee

description:
As a benefits administrator
  I want to edit existing employees
  So that employee details stay accurate

steps.name: I am authenticated on the benefits dashboard
I edit the existing employee through the UI modal
the employee row should appear in the table with the expected values and business-rule calculations

elements.name: [UI-F-006] Cancel edit should not persist changes

steps.name: I am authenticated on the benefits dashboard
I open edit and cancel for the existing employee
the existing employee should remain unchanged in the table
I am authenticated on the benefits dashboard
I open the add employee modal
the add button should be visible and update button hidden
I open the edit modal for the existing employee
the update button should be visible and add button hidden

elements.name: [UI-F-015] Edit modal pre-populates selected employee values

steps.name: I am authenticated on the benefits dashboard
I open the edit modal for the existing employee
the edit modal should show the selected employee values

name: Edit Employee

description:
As a benefits administrator
  I want a full happy path check of employee management
  So that core UI behavior is validated end to end

elements.name: [SMK-F-001] E2E happy path for add, edit, and delete employee

steps.name: I am on the Paylocity login page
I login with configured credentials
I should see the benefits dashboard
I add a new employee through the UI modal
the employee row should appear in the table with the expected values and business-rule calculations
the dashboard should remain in a valid state
I open the edit modal for the created employee
the edit modal should show the created employee values
I edit the existing employee through the UI modal
the employee row should appear in the table with the expected values and business-rule calculations
the dashboard should remain in a valid state
I delete the existing employee and confirm
the employee should not be visible in the table by id
the table should show no employees message
the dashboard should remain in a valid state

name: Dashboard Smoke

description:
As a security-focused QA engineer
  I want auth validation coverage
  So that unauthorized access is blocked

elements.name: [API-F-013] Authentication is required for employee endpoints

steps.name: I use authentication variation "missing"
I request all employees with the selected auth variation
auth outcome should be "rejected"

elements.name: [API-F-013] Authentication is required for employee endpoints

steps.name: I use authentication variation "invalid-token"
I request all employees with the selected auth variation
auth outcome should be "rejected"

elements.name: [API-F-013] Authentication is required for employee endpoints

steps.name: I use authentication variation "wrong-scheme"
I request all employees with the selected auth variation
auth outcome should be "rejected"

elements.name: [API-F-013] Authentication is required for employee endpoints

steps.name: I use authentication variation "valid-basic"
I request all employees with the selected auth variation
auth outcome should be "accepted"

name: Employees API Authentication

description:
As a QA engineer
  I want employee creation validations to be reliable
  So that new employees are created with valid data

steps.name: I have a valid employee payload
I create the employee via API
the employee is created successfully
the created employee payroll should match business rules
I have an invalid employee payload missing "firstName"
I create the employee via API
the API response should be a client error
I have an invalid employee payload missing "lastName"
I create the employee via API
the API response should be a client error
I have an invalid employee payload missing "username"
I create the employee via API
the API response should be a client error

I create the employee via API
the API response should be "rejected"
I have a payload with "firstName" length 1
I create the employee via API
the API response should be "accepted"

I create the employee via API
the API response should be "accepted"
I have a payload with "firstName" length 51
I create the employee via API
the API response should be "rejected"
I have a payload with "lastName" length 1
I create the employee via API
the API response should be "accepted"

the API response should be "accepted"
I have a payload with "lastName" length 51
I create the employee via API
the API response should be "rejected"

the API response should be "accepted"

I create the employee via API
the API response should be "accepted"
I have a payload with "username" length 51
I create the employee via API
the API response should be "rejected"

I create the employee via API
the API response should be "rejected"
payroll business rules should be respected for dependants outcome "rejected"
I have a valid employee payload with dependants 0
I create the employee via API
the API response should be "accepted"
payroll business rules should be respected for dependants outcome "accepted"
I have a valid employee payload with dependants 1
I create the employee via API
the API response should be "accepted"
payroll business rules should be respected for dependants outcome "accepted"
I have a valid employee payload with dependants 31
I create the employee via API
the API response should be "accepted"
payroll business rules should be respected for dependants outcome "accepted"
I have a valid employee payload with dependants 32
I create the employee via API
the API response should be "accepted"
payroll business rules should be respected for dependants outcome "accepted"
I have a valid employee payload with dependants 33
I create the employee via API
the API response should be "rejected"
payroll business rules should be respected for dependants outcome "rejected"
I have a payload attempting to control read-only field "gross"
I create the employee via API
read-only enforcement should be respected for "gross"
I have a payload attempting to control read-only field "benefitsCost"
I create the employee via API

I create the employee via API

I create the employee via API

I create the employee via API
expiration handling outcome should be "accepted"
I have a payload with expiration value "tomorrow"
I create the employee via API
expiration handling outcome should be "rejected"
I have a payload with expiration value "2026-13-99T99:99:99Z"
I create the employee via API

I have a payload with expiration value "2026-03-24"
I create the employee via API
expiration handling outcome should be "implementation-specific"

name: Employees API Create

description:
As a QA engineer
  I want employee deletion behavior to be reliable
  So that removed employees are no longer accessible

steps.name: an employee exists via API
I delete the seeded employee by id
I request employee by id
the API response should be a client error
I use invalid employee id "not-a-uuid"
I request "delete" with invalid employee id
the API response should be a client error
I use invalid employee id "12345"
I request "delete" with invalid employee id
the API response should be a client error
I request "delete" with invalid employee id
the API response should be a client error

name: Employees API Delete

description:
As a QA engineer
  I want employee retrieval behavior to be reliable
  So that employee data can be queried safely

elements.name: [API-F-002] Get all employees returns a valid list

steps.name: an employee exists via API
I request all employees
the employee list response should be successful
an employee exists via API
I request employee by id
the employee by id response should match seeded employee
the employee by id payroll should match business rules
I use invalid employee id "not-a-uuid"
I request "get" with invalid employee id
the API response should be a client error
I use invalid employee id "12345"
I request "get" with invalid employee id
the API response should be a client error
I request "get" with invalid employee id
the API response should be a client error

name: Employees API Read

description:
As a QA engineer
  I want smoke validation per CRUD operation
  So that failures are immediately visible by operation

steps.name: I have a valid employee payload
I create the employee via API
the employee is created successfully
the created employee payroll should match business rules
I request employee by id
the employee by id response should match seeded employee
I update the seeded employee with valid data
updated employee data should be persisted
updated employee payroll should match business rules
I delete the seeded employee by id
I request employee by id
the API response should be a client error

elements.name: [SMK-A-001] create uses POST /api/Employees and returns expected status

steps.name: API smoke prerequisites are ready for "create"
I execute API smoke operation "create"
API smoke operation "create" should return "200"
API smoke payroll should follow business rules for "create"
I attach API smoke trace for operation "create" using "POST" on "/api/Employees"

elements.name: [SMK-A-001] read uses GET /api/Employees/{id} and returns expected status

steps.name: API smoke prerequisites are ready for "read"
I execute API smoke operation "read"
API smoke operation "read" should return "200"
API smoke payroll should follow business rules for "read"
I attach API smoke trace for operation "read" using "GET" on "/api/Employees/{id}"

elements.name: [SMK-A-001] update uses PUT /api/Employees and returns expected status

steps.name: API smoke prerequisites are ready for "update"
I execute API smoke operation "update"
API smoke operation "update" should return "200"
API smoke payroll should follow business rules for "update"
I attach API smoke trace for operation "update" using "PUT" on "/api/Employees"

elements.name: [SMK-A-001] delete uses DELETE /api/Employees/{id} and returns expected status

steps.name: API smoke prerequisites are ready for "delete"
I execute API smoke operation "delete"
API smoke operation "delete" should return "200-or-204"
API smoke payroll should follow business rules for "delete"
I attach API smoke trace for operation "delete" using "DELETE" on "/api/Employees/{id}"

name: Employees API Smoke

description:
As a QA engineer
  I want employee update behavior to be reliable
  So that employee data stays consistent after changes

steps.name: an employee exists via API
I update the seeded employee with valid data
updated employee data should be persisted
updated employee payroll should match business rules

name: Employees API Update
